title: Tencent Collaborates with Chinagoods to Launch AI Navigation, Covering 80,000 Shops in Yiwu for Smart Store Search
excerpt: On July 20, Tencent Smart Retail and Tencent Maps jointly launched "Chinagoods AI Navigation" with the Chinagoods platform, implementing the industry's first mu
content_html:
On July 20, Tencent Smart Retail and Tencent Maps jointly launched "Chinagoods AI Navigation" with the Chinagoods platform, implementing the industry's first multi-building, cross-bridge indoor and outdoor integrated navigation solution in Yiwu, providing global buyers with AI route planning, indoor and outdoor navigation, and AI product recognition and store search services.

The Yiwu International Trade City has a total operating area of more than 8 million square meters, with 80,000 business units and 2.1 million products. Due to multiple buildings connected by corridors and passages, traditional navigation is difficult to cover complex spaces, and buyers have long faced problems such as difficulty finding stores and inaccurate route planning.

Based on the Tencent Maps navigation engine, the WeChat mini program "Xiaoshang AI Navigation" can automatically plan routes based on merchant names, business numbers, or product information, achieving accurate navigation across regions and floors. For example, buyers can walk from the third floor of Area 1 directly to the second floor of Area 4 through the corridor without asking for directions, and find the target store directly. Test data shows that the efficiency of finding stores has improved by nearly 70% compared to traditional methods.

In addition, this service integrates Chinagoods' AI product recognition capabilities, allowing buyers to match products and sources by taking a photo and navigate directly to the corresponding store. It also supports multilingual operation, building a complete link between online product selection and offline purchasing.

For merchants, the WeChat location card function helps convert online traffic into offline transactions. In the future, Tencent and Chinagoods will continue to expand the application of LBS and AI technologies in large-scale commercial scenarios, creating a replicable intelligent commercial navigation solution.

sentences.zh: 7月20日，腾讯智慧零售与腾讯地图联合中华货品平台推出“中华货品AI导航”，在义乌落地业界首个多层楼宇、跨桥室内外一体化导航解决方案，为全球买家提供AI路线规划、室内外导航、AI商品识别及店铺搜索服务。

content_html_zh:
7月20日，腾讯智慧零售与腾讯地图联合中华货品平台推出“中华货品AI导航”，在义乌落地业界首个多层楼宇、跨桥室内外一体化导航解决方案，为全球买家提供AI路线规划、室内外导航、AI商品识别及店铺搜索服务。

义乌国际商贸城总经营面积超过800万平方米，拥有经营单元8万个、商品210万种。 由于多栋建筑通过走廊、通道连接，传统导航难以覆盖复杂空间，买家长期面临寻店难、路线规划不准确等问题。

基于腾讯地图导航引擎，微信小程序“小商AI导航”可以根据商户名称、商号或产品信息自动规划路线，实现跨区域、跨楼层的精准导航。 例如，买家可以从一区三楼通过走廊直接走到四区二楼，无需问路，直接找到目标门店。 测试数据显示，寻店效率较传统方法提升近70%。

此外，该服务还集成了Chinagoods的AI商品识别能力，买家可以通过拍照匹配商品和货源，并直接导航到对应的店铺。 还支持多语言操作，构建线上选品与线下采购的完整链接。

对于商户来说，微信位置卡功能有助于将线上流量转化为线下交易。 未来，腾讯与中华货品将继续拓展LBS和AI技术在大型商业场景的应用，打造可复制的智能商业导航解决方案。
